ordinary|ผลรอบนี้คุ้มจริงในแกนที่พังที่สุด: `innomcp chat` กลับมาตอบได้จริงแล้วทั้ง `normal` และ `multiagent/thinking` บน browser live stack ไม่ได้ติด smoke fallback แล้ว **findings** 1. critical: chat พังเพราะ `smoke_mode=1` ใน `innomcp-node/.env` กับ `.env.local` บังคับ `generalgate` ให้คืน `smoke_deterministic` กับผู้ใช้จริง 2. critical: ถึงตัด smoke ออกแล้ว chat ก็ยังพังต่อ เพราะ bootstrap ของ remote lane ไม่อ่าน `ollama_url` ทำให้ route init ผิดปลายทาง 3. critical: ปลายทาง `mdes` ที่ `ollama_url=https://ollama.mdes-innova.online` ตอบ `get /api/tags = 200` แต่ `post /api/chat = 404` จึงไม่ใช่ ollama-native chat endpoint แบบที่ `new ollama(...).chat()` ใช้อยู่ 4. high: local fallback เดิมก็ใช้ model ไม่ตรงของจริง (`qwen2.5:0.5b` แล้วต่อมา `gemma3:4b`) ทั้งที่ host จริงมี `qwen2.5-coder:7b` ใช้งานได้ 5. high: even after local fallback worked, budget เดิมสั้นเกินไป เลยโดน `timeout` ทั้งที่ model กำลังตอบจริง 6. medium: ui ยังรกและยังไม่ manus-like พอ แม้ core chat จะกลับมาทำงานแล้ว **สิ่งที่ผมแก้** - ตัด global smoke gate ออกจาก live chat path ใน [chat.ts](c:\users\user-nt\dev\innomcp\innomcp-node\src\routes\api\chat.ts) - แก้ remote bootstrap ให้ init อ่าน `ollama_url` / `ollama_remote_url` / token เดียวกับ mdes path จริงใน [chat.ts](c:\users\user-nt\dev\innomcp\innomcp-node\src\routes\api\chat.ts) - เปลี่ยน fast/local fallback lane ให้ใช้ model ที่พิสูจน์แล้วว่าตอบได้บน host จริง คือ `qwen2.5-coder:7b` ใน [chat.ts](c:\users\user-nt\dev\innomcp\innomcp-node\src\routes\api\chat.ts) - เพิ่ม local retry branch พร้อม timeout ใหญ่ขึ้นสำหรับ fallback path ใน [chat.ts](c:\users\user-nt\dev\innomcp\innomcp-node\src\routes\api\chat.ts) - ซ่อน inline `multiagentpanel / thinking report` จากโหมดปกติไว้ใน [chatpage.tsx](c:\users\user-nt\dev\innomcp\innomcp-next\src\app\components\chat\chatpage.tsx) เพื่อให้หน้า normal ไม่รกเกินจำเป็น **หลักฐาน live ที่ผ่าน** - backend typecheck ผ่าน: - `npm exec tsc -- --noemit -p innomcp-node/tsconfig.json` - frontend typecheck ผ่าน: - `npm exec tsc -- --noemit -p innomcp-next/tsconfig.json` - normal chat บน browser ที่ `http://localhost:3000/`: - ก่อนแก้: `generalgate.reason = smoke_deterministic` - กลางทาง: `error` - ต่อมา: `timeout` - หลังแก้ครบ: `generalgate.reason = ok` - model ที่ตอบจริง: `qwen2.5-coder:7b` - เวลา: ประมาณ `3.6s` - คำตอบจริงกลับมา ไม่ใช่ fallback ปลอบใจ - ordinary|**soma / strategic lead output** - **target capability:** mother becomes a self-improving orchestrator that can detect its own failures, decompose them into repair goals, spawn specialist squads, validate fixes, and retain lessons. - **core loop:** 1. observe failure: test error, runtime exception, bad answer, failed tool call, regression, user correction. 2. convert failure into a structured `bugreport`. 3. decompose into hypotheses and repair goals. 4. spawn squads with bounded ownership. 5. compare proposed fixes against evidence. 6. integrate only after verification passes. 7. store the correction pattern in memory. - **goal decomposition model:** - `goal`: desired end state. - `successcriteria`: measurable proof of completion. - `constraints`: safety, scope, permissions, runtime limits. - `subgoals`: ordered or parallel task dag. - `verificationplan`: tests, probes, evaluator checks. - `stopcondition`: complete, blocked, unsafe, or needs human authority. - **self-correction architecture:** - **mother:** owns strategy, decomposition, squad spawning, merge decisions. - **debugger squad:** isolates root cause and produces evidence. - **executor squad:** implements the smallest viable fix. - **verifier squad:** runs tests, regression checks, and adversarial probes. - **critic squad:** checks whether the fix solves the real issue or only masks it. - **memory writer:** records reusable lessons after success. - **repair protocol:** - never let a squad mutate global state without a scoped task. - require every fix to include evidence: failing-before, passing-after. - reject fixes without a clear causal link to the bug. - prefer rollback or quarantine over speculative broad rewrites. - escalate only for destructive, credentialed, production, or scope-changing actions. - **autonomous agency safeguards:** - bounded workspaces per squad. - max concurrency limit. - shared task ledger. - patch review before integration. - verification gate before declaring success. - memory update only after validated completion. - human approval for irreversible actions. - **implementation phases:** - **phase 1:** structured bug/goal/task schemas. - **phase 2:** decomposition planner that turns failures into subgoals. - **phase 3:** squad spawning with role-specific prompts and ownership. - **phase 4:** verification-first repair loop. - **phase 5:** self-memory and recurring bug prevention. - **phase 6:** continuous autonomous improvement loop. - **acceptance criteria:** - mother detects a failing behavior automatically. - mother creates a repair dag with success criteria. - at least two squads work independently on diagnosis and verification. - a fix is accepted only with passing evidence. - failed fixes are rejected or retried with a new hypothesis. - the final lesson is stored for future prevention.

ordinary|findings: - `executephase(phasename, goal, context = '')`: mostly correct. separating prior context from `goal` is a sound fix if squad selection depends on goal keywords. minor prompt formatting issue: when context exists, `"provide your specialized output."` is appended directly after the context text on the same line, which can muddy the prompt. - decomposition filtering: risky. the new filter only accepts lines beginning with `1.`, `1)`, `-`, or `*`. if the decomposer returns valid phase lines like `phase 1: analyze...` or plain newline-separated phases, this now falls back to a single phase. that is a behavioral regression unless the upstream prompt strictly guarantees those markers. - phased execution summaries/error stop: directionally correct. stopping on a phase error is safer than silently continuing, and carrying a longer artifact improves dependent phases. the replacement `failed ? ${res.error}` appears to contain a corrupted character where an arrow/dash was intended, but that is cosmetic. - cli `run` parsing: regression. previously `node mother.js run "<goal>" 3` worked per the documented `[n]` syntax. the new parser removes that compatibility and only supports `--phases n` / `-n n`. even if intentional, it breaks existing callers/scripts. verdict: fail

ordinary|findings: 1. `pingbridge()` reports 404/401/3xx as up: `resolve({ up: res.statuscode < 500, ... })` means a missing `/gui`, auth failure, redirect, or wrong service on that port is considered healthy. that is a real false-green for the bridge check. it should likely require `2xx` or a known expected status/body. 2. git push gate can give a stale reading: `git rev-list --count origin/main..head` does not fetch first, so it only compares against the local `origin/main` ref. if that ref is stale, the diagnostic can misreport sync/ahead state. acceptable if “cached git state” is intended, but false as a push gate. no obvious crash path in the main json/event handling; malformed json lines are ignored safely. verdict: fail

ordinary|finding: `iserrorreply()` accepts any standalone `ok` before checking error sentinels. a reply like `not ok`, `ok: backend error`, or `[system override]: local ai query failed, ok` is classified `alive`, preserving the false-green path this patch is meant to prevent. check error patterns first, and ideally require an exact/near-exact ok response for the probe. finding: replies that lack `ok` but also lack one of the listed sentinel phrases are still treated as healthy. if the probe contract is “ask for ok; healthy reply contains ok,” then arbitrary garbage like `hello` or model chatter should not be `alive`. verdict: fail

ordinary|finding: `limbs/mother-engine.js:229-233` now trusts `r.attempts[].ok` over the previous non-empty reply check. in `model-router.js`, a backend attempt is marked `{ ok: true }` on any callback without `err`, even if `reply` is empty/whitespace/null. before this diff, such a result was recorded as failed by motherengine. now, whenever `attempts` is present, the final backend can be recorded as a success despite producing no usable answer. that weakens reliability scoring for malformed/empty model responses. fix direction: when recording attempts, override the final successful attempt’s `ok` with the existing `succeeded` check, or have `callmodel` only mark success when the reply is a usable non-empty string. verdict: fail

ordinary|findings: 1. **config parsing can break the breaker semantics.** `number(process.env.breaker_threshold || 3)` accepts `0`, negative values, `nan`, and fractional values. `breaker_threshold=0` opens a lane after the first failure check forever-ish behavior; `nan` disables opening entirely. same for cooldown: `nan` makes `_breakeropen()` always false, negative cooldown immediately closes. this should be clamped/validated. 2. **half-open behavior is coarse but acceptable.** after cooldown, `_breakeropen()` returns false and the next normal rotation call probes the lane. success closes; failure reopens. there is no single-flight half-open guard, so concurrent requests after cooldown can all hammer the recovering provider before the first failure reopens it. if concurrency is meaningful here, that is a real edge case. 3. **`norotate` bypass is mostly correct.** it bypasses the skip, so probes can test an open lane. success closes the breaker. failure reopens/keeps it open. that matches the stated intent. 4. **all-lanes-open behavior is intentional but harsh.** rotation will skip every open lane and return exhausted without trying any backend. that is consistent with “skip during cooldown,” but it means the router can fail fast even if one provider has recovered before its cooldown expires. not necessarily a bug unless availability is prioritized over budget protection. 5. **`date.now()` use is acceptable for this scope.** wall-clock jumps can extend or shorten cooldowns, but for a simple in-process breaker this is usually tolerable. monotonic time would be cleaner, not required. the only concrete blocker is unvalidated env parsing; the rest are design tradeoffs unless this router has high concurrency. verdict: fail

ordinary|finding: `_savebreaker()` is non-atomic and uncoordinated. concurrent one-shot cli processes can lose each other’s breaker updates via last-writer-wins, and a reader can observe a truncated/partial write. since `_loadbreaker()` treats any parse/read failure as `{}`, one partial write can temporarily erase all open breakers and defeat the feature’s main purpose. finding: expired pruning only happens in memory on load. the file is never rewritten after pruning, so stale entries can remain indefinitely unless a later trip/reset happens. that is less severe, but it means the persisted file is not actually kept clean as claimed. file i/o failure is currently silent best-effort. that may be acceptable operationally, but it also means missing `network/`, permission errors, or disk failures make persistence silently vanish with no doctor-visible warning. verdict: fail
